name: pricing
context: Per-model token pricing, batch discounts, prompt caching costs, tool pricing, and cost optimization strategies
info: Anthropic bills per token — input and output tokens are priced separately for each model. Key pricing dimensions: base input/output rates, prompt caching (writes at 1.25x or 2x, reads at 0.1x), batch API (50% discount), fast mode (6x for Opus 4.6), data residency (1.1x for US-only inference), and tool-specific costs (web search at $10/1000 searches, web fetch free, code execution free with web tools). One token is approximately 4 characters or 0.75 words in English. Pricing discounts stack: batch + caching together provides maximum savings. Use the Anthropic Console Usage page to monitor spend.
triggers: how much does claude api cost
what is the pricing for claude
body:
# Pricing

All prices in USD per million tokens (MTok). One token ≈ 4 characters ≈ 0.75 words.

## Model Pricing

| Model | Input | Output | Batch Input | Batch Output |
|-------|-------|--------|-------------|-------------|
| **Opus 4.6** | $5/MTok | $25/MTok | $2.50/MTok | $12.50/MTok |
| **Sonnet 4.6** | $3/MTok | $15/MTok | $1.50/MTok | $7.50/MTok |
| **Haiku 4.5** | $1/MTok | $5/MTok | $0.50/MTok | $2.50/MTok |
| Opus 4.5 | $5/MTok | $25/MTok | $2.50/MTok | $12.50/MTok |
| Opus 4.1 | $15/MTok | $75/MTok | $7.50/MTok | $37.50/MTok |
| Sonnet 4.5 | $3/MTok | $15/MTok | $1.50/MTok | $7.50/MTok |
| Sonnet 4 | $3/MTok | $15/MTok | $1.50/MTok | $7.50/MTok |
| Haiku 3.5 | $0.80/MTok | $4/MTok | $0.40/MTok | $2/MTok |

## Prompt Caching Pricing

| Model | Base Input | 5m Cache Write | 1h Cache Write | Cache Read |
|-------|-----------|----------------|----------------|------------|
| Opus 4.6 | $5/MTok | $6.25/MTok | $10/MTok | $0.50/MTok |
| Sonnet 4.6 | $3/MTok | $3.75/MTok | $6/MTok | $0.30/MTok |
| Haiku 4.5 | $1/MTok | $1.25/MTok | $2/MTok | $0.10/MTok |

**Cache pricing multipliers:**
- 5-minute cache write: 1.25x base input
- 1-hour cache write: 2x base input
- Cache read: 0.1x base input (90% discount)

Break-even: 1 read for 5m cache, 2 reads for 1h cache. See [[anthropic/prompt-caching]].

## Discount Stacking

Discounts compose multiplicatively:

| Scenario | Input Cost (Sonnet 4.6) |
|----------|------------------------|
| Standard | $3.00/MTok |
| Batch only | $1.50/MTok |
| Cache read only | $0.30/MTok |
| Batch + cache read | $0.15/MTok |

## Tool-Specific Pricing

| Tool | Cost |
|------|------|
| **Web search** | $10 per 1,000 searches + standard token costs |
| **Web fetch** | Free (standard token costs only) |
| **Code execution** | Free with web tools; otherwise $0.05/hr/container after 1,550 free hours/month |
| **Bash tool** | +245 input tokens per call |
| **Text editor** | +700 input tokens per call |
| **Tool use overhead** | 313-346 tokens system prompt per request (when tools provided) |

## Fast Mode (Opus 4.6 Only)

| | Input | Output |
|--|-------|--------|
| Fast mode | $30/MTok | $150/MTok |

6x standard pricing. Stacks with caching and data residency multipliers. Not available with batch.

## Data Residency

US-only inference (`inference_geo: "us"`) adds a 1.1x multiplier on all token categories for Opus 4.6+ models. Global routing (default) uses standard pricing.

## Cost Estimation Examples

### Chat Application (Sonnet 4.6)
- Average conversation: 2K input tokens, 500 output tokens
- Cost per conversation: (2000 × $3 + 500 × $15) / 1,000,000 = $0.0135
- 10,000 conversations/day: ~$135/day

### With Prompt Caching (80% cache hit)
- 1,600 tokens cached read: 1600 × $0.30 / 1M = $0.00048
- 400 tokens uncached: 400 × $3 / 1M = $0.0012
- 500 output tokens: 500 × $15 / 1M = $0.0075
- Total: $0.00918/conversation (32% savings)

### Batch Processing (Sonnet 4.6)
- 100K documents, 1K tokens each input, 500 tokens output
- Input: 100M × $1.50 / 1M = $150
- Output: 50M × $7.50 / 1M = $375
- Total: $525 (vs $1,050 standard — 50% savings)

## Monitoring Spend

- **Console**: [console.anthropic.com/usage](https://console.anthropic.com/usage) for real-time usage
- **API**: Token counts in every response's `usage` field
- **Workspace limits**: Set per-workspace spend caps to prevent overuse

## Cost Optimization Strategies

1. **Choose the right model** — Haiku for simple tasks, Sonnet for most work, Opus for complex reasoning
2. **Use prompt caching** — 90% discount on repeated content
3. **Use batch API** — 50% discount for non-time-sensitive work
4. **Stack discounts** — Batch + caching for maximum savings
5. **Minimize tool overhead** — Cache tool definitions
6. **Set workspace limits** — Prevent accidental overspend
7. **Monitor usage** — Track cost per conversation/request

sections.title: Per-model pricing
sections.body: Current flagship models: Opus 4.6 ($5/$25 in/out), Sonnet 4.6 ($3/$15), Haiku 4.5 ($1/$5). All per million tokens. Legacy models vary. Opus 4.1 and Opus 4 are significantly more expensive at $15/$75. Use the cheapest model that meets your quality requirements.

sections.title: Prompt caching costs
sections.body: 5-minute cache write: 1.25x base input. 1-hour write: 2x base. Cache reads: 0.1x base (90% off). Break-even after 1 read for 5m cache, 2 reads for 1h. Cache reads don't count toward ITPM rate limits, providing both cost and throughput benefits. See [[anthropic/prompt-caching]].

sections.title: Batch API discount
sections.body: 50% discount on both input and output tokens for all models. Batch processing is asynchronous (up to 24h). Stacks with caching discounts. Example: Sonnet 4.6 batch + cache read = $0.15/MTok input (95% off standard). Best for non-time-sensitive bulk work. See [[anthropic/batch]].

sections.title: Cost optimization strategies
sections.body: 1. Right-size model selection (Haiku for simple, Sonnet for most, Opus for complex). 2. Cache repeated content (system prompts, docs, tools). 3. Batch non-urgent work. 4. Stack discounts (batch + caching). 5. Monitor usage in the Console. 6. Set workspace spend limits. 7. Minimize prompt size by being concise.

related.note: Caching provides 90% discount on cached reads

author: dojo-community
